revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nagatsukakofun}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nagatukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Kani, Gifu]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Nagatsukakofun}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nagatukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kani, Gifu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[burial mound]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue ware]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|25|52|N|137|04|07|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kani, Gifu]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[Kani, Gifu]] in the [[Chubu region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].

The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[Kani Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Taita Line]].

Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.

This was confirmed by the [[grave goods]] found within the [[burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11651651]] Wikidata name: Nagatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|qid=Q853805}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kani Station|Kani Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Taita Line|Taita Line]].

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kani Station|Kani Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Taita Line|Taita Line]].

The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kani Station|Kani Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Taita Line|Taita Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11651651]] Wikidata name: Nagatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|WD=Q853805}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kani Station|Kani Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Taita Line|Taita Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11651651]] Wikidata name: Nagatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|WD=Q853805}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kani Station|Kani Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Taita Line|Taita Line]].
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11651651]] Wikidata name: Nagatsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|WD=Q853805}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kani, Gifu|Kani, Gifu]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a [[:en:Fluvial terrace|river terrace]] between the [[:en:Kiso River|Kiso River]] and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Kani Station|Kani Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Taita Line|Taita Line]].

Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|WD=Q853805}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kani, Gifu|en|Kani, Gifu|WD=Q853805|lt=Kani, Gifu}} in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}} between the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River}} and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kani Station|en|Kani Station|WD=Q6363050|lt=Kani Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Taita Line|en|Taita Line|WD=Q870995|lt=Taita Line}}.
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|WD=Q853805}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kani, Gifu|en|Kani, Gifu|WD=Q853805|lt=Kani, Gifu}} in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace}} between the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River}} and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kani Station|en|Kani Station|WD=Q6363050|lt=Kani Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Taita Line|en|Taita Line|WD=Q870995|lt=Taita Line}}.
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|WD=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nagatsukakofun}} *[https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/siseki/nagatukakofunn.html Gifu Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|WD=Q853805|1=Kani, Gifu}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kani, Gifu|en|Kani, Gifu|WD=Q853805|lt=Kani, Gifu|1=Kani, Gifu}} in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|WD=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} between the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}} and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kani Station|en|Kani Station|WD=Q6363050|lt=Kani Station|1=Kani Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Taita Line|en|Taita Line|WD=Q870995|lt=Taita Line|1=Taita Line}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Nagatsuka Kofun (Kani)]][[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nagatsuka Kofun]][[ja:長塚古墳 (可児市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Naghhhh | native_name =長塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nagatsuka Kofun, Nakaedo Kani 2018.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nagatsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.43111111|N|137.06861111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kani|qid=Q853805|1=Kani, Gifu}} | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nagatsuka Kofun'''|長塚古墳||}} is a [[kofun]] [[Kofun]] located in the Nakaedo neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kani, Gifu|en|Kani, Gifu|qid=Q853805|lt=Kani, Gifu|1=Kani, Gifu}} in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection extended in 2003. <ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162791|title= 長塚古墳|trans-title=Nagatsuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nagatsuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is constructed in two-tiers and is located at an elevation of 10 meters on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} between the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|qid=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}} and its tributary, the Kanogawa River. It is the largest of the Maenami cluster of 15 tumuli in the same area. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] by the Kani City Department of Education in 1982, 1986 and from 1995 to 1997. The tumulus was found to be 81 meters long, with a posterior circular portion 46 meters in diameter, 8.3 meters high, and a 25 meter wide by 5.9 meter high anterior rectangular portion. Neither ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were discovered, but the tumulus had traces of a five-meter wide moat on its south and east sides.
The tumulus was estimated to be from the latter half of the 4th century based on its construction style. This was confirmed by the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found within the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, one of which was located in the anterior and one in the posterior portions of the mound. The grave goods included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s , glass balls, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery, and fragments of a wooden coffin.
The excavated relics are exhibited at the {{nihongo|'''Kani Local History Museum'''|可児郷土歴史館|Kani Kyōdo Rekishikan}}. The tumulus is located about a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Kani Station|en|Kani Station|qid=Q6363050|lt=Kani Station|1=Kani Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Taita Line|en|Taita Line|qid=Q870995|lt=Taita Line|1=Taita Line}}.
==Gallery== <gallery> Nagatsukakofun-3.JPG|Marker Nagatsuka kofun - 1.jpg|side view </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|qid=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
